fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: West Virginia Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to West Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity west virginia, how to transfer dietitian license to west virginia, dietitian compact west virginia, west virginia dietitian endorsement application

board.name: West Virginia Board of Licensed Dietitians (WVBOLD)

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Annual + 20 CE hours every two years

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice and provide medical nutrition therapy in West Virginia, regulated by WVBOLD.

license_types.title: Provisional Dietitian Permit
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid while awaiting examination and allows practice under supervision. Costs $46.

steps.title: Complete Required Education and Supervised Practice
steps.description: Complete a minimum of a baccalaureate degree from a regionally accredited US college or university and complete a Commission on Accreditation for Dietetics Education (ACDE) accredited Dietetic Internship or its equivalent.

steps.title: Pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians
steps.description: Successfully pass the Registration Examination for Dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Apply for West Virginia Dietitian Licensure
steps.description: Submit a completed application and all required documentation to the West Virginia Board of Licensed Dietitians (WVBOLD), including official transcripts, examination results, and payment of fees.

documents.detail: Application form filled out accurately and completely, confirming age and citizenship.
Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree (may be satisfied by CDR card, check with board).

provisional_text: West Virginia offers a provisional permit for $46 for individuals who possess a degree and have completed the supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. This permit allows the applicant to practice under the supervision of a licensed dietitian while preparing for the exam.
reciprocity_text: Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration or a current license in another state with equivalent standards may apply by submitting the standard application, fee, and proof of their active credential/license.

faqs.question: How much does a West Virginia dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $69. A provisional permit application costs $46.

faqs.question: When does the West Virginia license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: West Virginia dietitian licenses are renewed annually. The renewal fee is $69.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for West Virginia?
faqs.answer: To maintain licensure, West Virginia requires 20 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) every two years.

faqs.question: Does West Virginia have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: West Virginia has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to West Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in West Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration or a current license in another state with equivalent standards may apply by submitting the standard application, fee, and proof of their active credential/license.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in West Virginia
hero.dek: West Virginia Dietitian licensure requires completing an accredited dietetics program, passing the Registration Examination for Dietitians, and meeting continuing education requirements.
